title: Amazon Bedrock & Agent Core Developer Course

timeline.label: Introduction to Serverless AI
timeline.text: Welcome to the Amazon Bedrock course. Today we're learning serverless AI, starting from the basic foundations of what foundation models are and how AWS handles hosting.

timeline.label: Navigating the Playground
timeline.text: Let's open the AWS Console model playgrounds. You'll see how to select Claude, Nova, or Llama and test queries instantly without writing any code.

timeline.label: Controlling Personas (System Prompts)
timeline.text: System prompts set the rules and persona for the assistant. Here we'll configure system guidelines to limit response scopes and secure behavior.

timeline.label: Programmatic Inference with Converse API
timeline.text: To invoke models from Python, we call the converse endpoint in boto3. This provides a unified request-response interface.

timeline.label: Inference Configurations (Temperature & Tokens)
timeline.text: We tune temperature to adjust randomness, and max tokens to control output length. A temperature of 0.0 guarantees exact, repeatable JSON outputs.

timeline.label: State in Multi-Turn Chats
timeline.text: LLMs are stateless. To support an ongoing chat, we must preserve and append the messages history array on each invocation.

timeline.label: Tokens & Billing Optimization
timeline.text: Calculating token metrics is critical for cost. We look at input vs output tokens and discuss sliding window history pruning.

timeline.label: Tool Use (Function Calling)
timeline.text: Tool Use lets models trigger local APIs. We define input JSON schemas and implement the two-step request-execution loop.

timeline.label: Vector Embeddings & RAG Ingestion
timeline.text: We chunk documents, create Titan Text Embeddings, index them in a vector database, and perform semantic document retrieval.

timeline.label: Bedrock Guardrails & PII Masking
timeline.text: Safety filter configuration. We mask PII, apply custom blocklists, and evaluate inputs before forwarding requests.

timeline.label: Bedrock Agent Architectures
timeline.text: Overview of building fully autonomous agents with action groups and knowledge base integrations.

timeline.label: Bedrock Agent Core
timeline.text: Deep dive into Agent runtime orchestrators, memory persistence, tracing reasoning steps, and configuring IAM policies.
